_meta.name: CLM r5 GPU dispatch smoke result — 2026-04-20
_meta.purpose: End-to-end verification that HXBLAS_CUDA=1 routes train_clm matmul through cuBLAS on H100, with numerical diff vs CPU baseline and wallclock comparison.
_meta.session: task #6 + r5-a10.5-D — CLM 1B GPU unblock investigation
_meta.trainer_binary: build/artifacts/train_clm @ commit afb15ad (built H100 2026-04-19 18:22 UTC, pre-r5-a10 leak fix)

verdict: HXBLAS_CUDA=1 wires correctly — cuBLAS engages with hundreds of real sgemm calls per step. Numerical output bit-identical to CPU OpenBLAS. BUT speedup is only 1.037x (3.7%) because train_clm.hexa per-call list↔raw marshal (alloc_raw + _list_to_raw + _raw_to_list + free_raw) dominates wallclock — cuBLAS sgemm itself completes in microseconds while marshal of M·K + K·N + M·N floats from hexa list to C f32 buffer takes seconds. GPU memory util peaks at 0 MiB (nvidia-smi 1Hz polling misses the brief device alloc/copy windows; sm util peaks at 1% twice per step).

dispatch_wire.linux_resolution_mechanism: hexa_ffi_dlopen() in hexa-lang/self/runtime.c falls back to libhxblas.so via LD_LIBRARY_PATH search when .dylib path fails
dispatch_wire.fix_applied_h100: ln -sf libhxblas_cuda.so /workspace/anima/training/build/libhxblas.so (symlink swap; original CPU lib backed up to libhxblas.so.cpu_backup_20260420)
dispatch_wire.silent_fallback_audit: Without LD_LIBRARY_PATH set: hexa-ffi dlsym fails for hxblas_sgemm + hxblas_attn_softmax_causal + hxlmhead_bwd + hxlmhead_version (4 symbols). dlopen(NULL) returns main exec which lacks all four. Trainer continues with NULL ffi pointers. ROOT CAUSE: trainer must be launched with LD_LIBRARY_PATH explicitly set.

gpu_run.cublas_call_count_step1_b1_s128: 300+ (verified by stderr counter — gemm shapes M=512 N=768 K=768 for projections, M=512 N=512 K=96 for attention)

root_cause_analysis.why_no_speedup: cuBLAS sgemm is engaged (300+ calls/step, real shapes) and produces correct results, but the per-call envelope is dominated by hexa-side marshal (alloc_raw + _list_to_raw memcpy from hexa list ints/floats to f32 buffer + cudaMalloc + cudaMemcpyAsync H2D + cublasSgemm + cudaMemcpyAsync D2H + cudaStreamSynchronize + cudaFree + _raw_to_list + free_raw). The marshal cost is O(M·K + K·N + M·N) floats per call and runs single-threaded in the hexa interpreter loop. cublasSgemm itself completes in <1ms for these shapes on H100.
evidence: GPU sm util peaked at 1% twice per step (= cuBLAS handle init + workspace alloc, not sustained compute)
GPU memory util reads 0 MiB at 1Hz — device buffers are alloc'd and freed within sub-second windows per call
1.037x speedup with 300+ cuBLAS calls per step indicates sgemm time is <0.1% of wallclock
Per-call cudaMalloc/Free pattern in shim (lines 189-194 + 226) burns PCIe + driver overhead per matmul
leverage_options_for_real_speedup: Option A (preferred): persistent device buffer pool in shim — eliminate cudaMalloc/Free per call. Reuse single workspace per shape class. Est 5-10x reduction in cuBLAS path overhead.
Option B: hexa-side alloc_raw → cudaMallocManaged so marshal writes directly to UVM. Eliminates H2D copy. Est 30-50% reduction.
Option C: batch fuse multiple sgemm calls (gemm_strided_batched). Requires train_clm.hexa restructure to expose batched matmul. Out of scope this iteration.
Option D: replace per-call list↔raw with persistent raw arena across whole step. Largest single lever — would attack the dominant cost (marshal, not GPU).

decision.170m_full_gpu_launch_reason: 1.037x speedup vs CPU does not justify GPU spend. CPU hetzner path at 90s/step is cheaper than H100 at 166s/step. Hardware idle but tooling cannot leverage it without shim refactor (Option A) or marshal elimination (Option D).
decision.1b_gpu_launch_reason: Same marshal bottleneck applies at 1B scale; GPU would still be idle while CPU marshals. Estimated >1500s/step at 1B with current dispatch.
decision.h100_pod_action: KEEP_RUNNING — pod 87xscivuggrwvk left intact. libhxblas_cuda.so + symlink + corpus symlink installed for next iteration. No new pod fired. Authorization respected (1 of 2 allowed pods).

r5_a6_6_persistent_device_pool.title: hxblas_cuda_shim.c — replace per-call cudaMalloc/Free with size-class pool
r5_a6_6_persistent_device_pool.estimated_improvement: 5-10x cuBLAS path overhead reduction → expected 3-8x e2e speedup if marshal is also addressed

r5_a10_5_E_marshal_elimination.title: train_clm.hexa _matmul/_matmul_At — persistent step-scoped raw arena
r5_a10_5_E_marshal_elimination.scope: training/train_clm.hexa _matmul/_matmul_At — keep buf_A/B/C across calls within a step, free at step end
r5_a10_5_E_marshal_elimination.owner: parallel BG already addressing list-op leak (r5-a10.5-A native zero-fill)

next_actions.ubu_status: SKIPPED — host unreachable (LAN ssh refused, tailscale timeout). H100 used as primary GPU host this iteration per feedback_h100_free_use + pod was idle 0%/0MiB.

policy_compliance.feedback_ai_native_clm: OBSERVED — hxblas FFI is pure C+cuBLAS, no Python/Rust, no quantization, hexa-native dispatch wired
policy_compliance.feedback_no_quantization: OBSERVED — fp32 throughout shim and training
policy_compliance.feedback_h100_authorized: OBSERVED — used existing pod 87xscivuggrwvk (1 of 2 cap), no new pod fired
policy_compliance.feedback_h100_free_use: OBSERVED — leveraged idle pod, no kill, ALM r11 path (if any) untouched (verified ps aux: only sshd + start.sh + nginx + sleep, no python/training process)
policy_compliance.feedback_no_resume_data_change: N/A — smoke only, no production launch initiated
policy_compliance.feedback_one_shot_best: OBSERVED — no premature 1B launch; smoke FAIL gate honored
policy_compliance.feedback_py_ban_total: OBSERVED — zero .py created/edited/run on Mac or H100

blockers_remaining_for_1b: B1: cuBLAS shim per-call cudaMalloc/Free dominates GPU envelope (Option A above)
B2: hexa-side _matmul marshal (alloc_raw + _list_to_raw) dominates total step wallclock (Option D above) — even fixing B1 alone won't unblock 1B unless B2 also addressed

B3 (informational): pre-built train_clm binary @ afb15ad lacks r5-a10 leak fix; before any production launch, rebuild with HEAD train_clm.hexa (blocked on hexa compiler not installed on H100 pod — would need scp from Mac or rebuild via hexa_v2 binary)

revision_history.event: session start — task #6 unblock CLM 1B GPU path
ubu LAN ssh refused, tailscale timeout. ubu2 has no GPU/CUDA. Pivot to H100 pod 87xscivuggrwvk (idle, name=clm_r5_h100, allowed by feedback_h100_authorized).
Installed libopenblas-dev on H100. Built libhxblas_cuda.so (30808 bytes, links libcublas.so.12 + libopenblas.so.0). Smoke basic + extended PASS.

Symlink libhxblas.so → libhxblas_cuda.so, corpus symlink. First trainer smoke under HXBLAS_CUDA=1: cuBLAS handle ready msg appears but GPU 0/0 throughout. Suspected silent fallback. Patched shim with stderr gemm counter — confirmed 300+ real cuBLAS calls per step.

Reverted trace patch. Clean A/B comparison: GPU 499.8s vs CPU 518.5s for 3 steps batch=1 seq=128. speedup=1.037x, loss_diff=0. Verdict: dispatch correct, but marshal dominates → not the leverage point.
